finding_results.notes: Three changes made to break thesis-speech pattern in the post-Red-Reserve-reveal section. (1) Vel's balanced aphorism ('Untrained three-hundreds break whatever they hit. Trained two-fifties choose where it breaks.') replaced with a concrete tactical assessment referencing Sable's team's failure and ending with a glance at Dren's injured arm — threat is specific and immediate, not quotable. (2) Kem's clean system diagnosis ('So zero wasn't what scared them...') replaced with a pressured, self-correcting reaction that starts from his father's deterioration, breaks mid-sentence, and restarts quieter with a partial understanding ('They knew what was down there') rather than a finished political analysis. (3) Sable's thematic farewell ('what the last twenty-seven years were helping to keep shut') replaced with concrete items she needs to verify ('the threshold. The garrison.') and a personal coda ('Not from a file.'); commander warning compressed from a quotable flourish to blunt operational shorthand. Red Reserve threat, Sable's southbound choice, and all operational facts preserved. Kem's reaction includes explicit self-correction per acceptance test requirement.

summary: Single finding addressed with three targeted dialogue edits in the Red Reserve reveal sequence (lines 191-221). Vel's combat assessment, Kem's reaction to the garrison truth, and Sable's departure speech were all roughened from polished thesis-speech into character-specific pressured responses: Vel answers tactically with concrete stakes, Kem processes through his father's deterioration with a mid-sentence break, and Sable commits to going south without explaining the chapter's thematic significance to the room. All operational content, setups (sable_question, threshold_garrison), and the chapter's must-land beats preserved.
